workbookTracks.goal: Measure accuracy with balanced, high-confidence reference material
workbookTracks.note: The repository does not bundle those external corpora yet, so this run uses an internal proxy corpus aligned to that track.

workbookTracks.goal: Measure ambiguous readings, partial parses, and placeholder inference separately
workbookTracks.note: Exact-pass status is paired with detailed failure reasons because these cases can support multiple plausible readings.

workbookTracks.goal: Measure throughput, long-sequence latency, and symbol normalization robustness
workbookTracks.note: This run includes both mixed-corpus throughput and long-sequence latency.

failures.progression: D D7 | G | C/E C Am | D7/F# D

notes: licenseNotes=Source excerpts from DCMLab ABC (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0). Fixture provenance is documented in tool/benchmark_fixtures/external_gold/abc/README.md.

likelyRootCauses: Multiple key centers remain plausible after scoring, so the winner is sensitive to small cadence and weighting changes.
Applied-dominant function is present, but the displayed Roman token diverges from the annotation style expected by the benchmark.

failures.description: real modulation stays distinct from tonicization
failures.progression: Cmaj7 Dm7 G7 Cmaj7 | Em7 A7 | Dmaj7 Gmaj7 | A7 Dmaj7 | G7 Cmaj7
likelyRootCauses: The analyzer is detecting local key motion, but its global summary boundary between tonicization and true modulation is still too soft.
Multiple key centers remain plausible after scoring, so the winner is sensitive to small cadence and weighting changes.
Late cadential material appears to outweigh earlier home-key evidence in the primary key summary.
